Wire-format-conformant FULL ZK PROOF ground truth from google/longfellow-zk (conformance step C.8).

Produced by zk_anchor.cc, run inside the longfellow-ref Docker image. The dump is computed by the
reference implementation in its own build environment via development tooling outside this repository:

  docker run --rm \
    -v "<repo>/tempdocs/longfellow-zk-reference:/src" \
    -v "<repo>/tempdocs/longfellow-anchors:/anchor" \
    longfellow-ref bash -c \
    "g++ -O2 -std=c++17 -mpclmul -I /src/lib /anchor/zk_anchor.cc /src/lib/util/log.cc -lcrypto -o /tmp/a && /tmp/a"

The harness compiles the same small GF2_128<4> circuit C.7 used through the real compiler. The full
field is 16-byte GF(2^128) (matching the C.2-C.7 Ligero stack); the subfield is GF(2^16). The circuit
is the field-satisfiable relation w == (x + y) * (x + z) * x (x public, y/z/w private), chosen so
logc = 0 (no copies) and nl = 3 (a genuine multi-layer walk with per-layer logw of 2, 3, 3). The fixed
witnesses are x = of_scalar(3), y = of_scalar(5), z = of_scalar(7), w = the field product; W[0] is the
constant-one wire. The deterministic Fiat-Shamir seed is "zk8" (ASCII), the pad/commitment randomness
the counter RandomEngine (k-th byte = k & 0xFF), the Ligero rate (rateinv) 4 and nreq 2.

The harness runs the real ZkProver (commit + prove), serializes the whole ZkProof envelope
(write_com || write_sc_proof || write_com_proof), then runs the reference ZkVerifier::verify over the
parsed bytes. It dumps:

  - the circuit shape (nv, logv, nc, logc, nl, ninputs, npub_in, subfield_boundary; per-layer
    nw/logw/nterms; id);
  - the Quad wiring terms per layer (L<ly>_t<i>_g / _h0 / _h1 / _v): the (g, h0, h1, v) corners
    Quad::bind_gh_all iterates, in iteration order. v is to_bytes_field; g/h0/h1 are decimal indices;
  - the input element bytes (input0..input4, to_bytes_field) = the witness column W;
  - the derived LigeroParam (param_*): nw = (ninputs - npub_in) + pad_size(c), nq = nl, plus the full
    layout (block_enc, block, dblock, block_ext, r, w, nwrow, nqtriples, nwqrow, nrow); pad_size,
    n_witness;
  - the commitment root (com_root) and proved flag;
  - the full proof bytes (proof_bytes, proof_len) and the segment lengths (seg_com_len, seg_sc_len);
    com_proof_len = proof_len - seg_com_len - seg_sc_len;
  - the verifier_constraints output: cn (constraint count), the A sparse term count (vc_a_size /
    vc_a_terms), the b vector (vc_b<i>), and every A term (vc_a<i>_c, vc_a<i>_w, vc_a<i>_k);
  - the per-layer alpha/beta and hand challenges (vc_L<ly>_alpha / _beta / _hb0_<r> / _hb1_<r>) and the
    per-layer bind_quad / eqv / eqq (vc_L<ly>_bindquad / _eqv / _eqq); and
  - the reference ZkVerifier accept over the parsed full proof (ref_parsed, ref_zk_verify).

The verifier_constraints / bind_quad dumps are computed on a transcript that FIRST absorbs the
commitment root (LigeroVerifier::receive_commitment) THEN performs the Fiat-Shamir setup, exactly as the
real ZkVerifier orders recv_commitment before verify. The root absorb is what makes the dumped A/b match
the verifier's; a transcript without it derives a different (inconsistent) challenge stream.

verifier_constraints (lib/zk/zk_common.h) turns the sumcheck claims into the Ligero linear-constraint
system A*w = b over the committed witness (the private inputs followed by the proof pad). Per layer it
builds a symbolic Expression for the running sumcheck claim through ConstraintBuilder.first/next, then
finalize emits one constraint: the quadratic relation claim = eqq * W[R,C] * W[L,C] rearranged into
A*w = b form, where eqq = Eq[Q,C] * QUAD[R,L] = eqv * bind_quad. bind_quad (Quad::bind_gh_all) binds
the layer's Quad at the output point (g0, g1, alpha, beta) and the hand challenges (hb[0], hb[1]); eqv
(Eq::eval over logc copy variables) is 1 here since logc = 0. The pad layout (PadLayout) places each
layer's claim/poly pads at consecutive witness indices starting at n_witness (the first pad index pi);
adjacent layers' claim pads overlap. After the layers, input_constraint adds one more constraint
binding the witness inputs: <eq0 + alpha.eq1, witness> = (wc[0] + alpha*wc[1]) - public_binding, with
the public binding folded out using the public inputs.

The composition order the ZK verifier runs (lib/zk/zk_verifier.h):
  1. ZkCommon::initialize_sumcheck_fiat_shamir (id, public inputs, zero, nterms zero bytes);
  2. verifier_constraints: replay the sumcheck (begin_circuit Q/G; per layer begin_layer alpha/beta,
     the round-polynomial/challenge walk, the wc claims) building A/b, ending with input_alpha and the
     input constraint; returns cn;
  3. LigeroVerifier::verify with the circuit-derived LigeroParam, hash_of_A = {de,ad,be,ef}, the lqc
     (the per-layer claim-pad product triples setup_lqc lays out), the A/b system, and b.

The commitment root is absorbed (recv_commitment) BEFORE verify, exactly as the Ligero layer's
receive_commitment does its write_commitment; the sc segment and com_proof segment follow the C.6/C.7
wire layouts. The lqc quadratic constraints come from setup_lqc: per layer the claim pad triple
(x = claim_pad(0), y = claim_pad(1), z = claim_pad(2)) at consecutive pad indices, asserting the
product dWC[0]*dWC[1] = dWC[0]*dWC[1] over the committed pad.

The ZK PROVER side (our engine emitting this wire) and the mdoc envelope are above this segment and out
of scope for C.8.
